name: Danish Almond Crescent Ring
recipeIngredient: 3 1/2 ounce tube almond paste, broken into pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F.
2. Lightly grease a cookie sheet.
3. In a small bowl using a fork, combine the sugar, butter or margarine, and almond paste; mix well. Set aside.
4. Unroll the dough into two long rectangles. Overlap the long sides to form one large rectangle. Firmly press perforations to seal. Press or roll out to a 16x8-inch rectangle. Cut lengthwise into three equal strips.
5. Place about 3 tablespoonfuls of the filling mixture evenly down the center of each strip. Gently press filling to form a 1-inch wide strip.
6. Overlap the sides of the dough over the filling, firmly pinching the edges to seal.
7. On an ungreased cookie sheet, loosely braid the three filled strips. Form the braid into a ring and pinch the ends of the strips together to seal.
8. Brush with egg white; sprinkle with sugar and sliced almonds.
9. Bake at 375°F for 15-22 minutes or until golden brown. Cool for 5 minutes before removing from the cookie sheet. Serve warm.
8 servings
Per serving: 250 calories, 5 g protein, 24 g carbohydrate, 15 g fat, 35 mg cholesterol, 290 mg sodium
Pillsbury Classic Cookbook #86, 33rd Bake-Off, p.44
